title: Cast of a variable to an IRI does not match in BGP but works with filter
description:
PREFIX up:<http://purl.uniprot.org/core/>
SELECT ?start ?protein ?input
WHERE
{
	BIND('P05067' AS ?input)
	BIND(
      IF(strstarts(?input, 'PRO_'),
      	IRI(CONCAT('http://purl.uniprot.org/annotation/', ?input)),
        IF (contains(?input, '-'),
            IRI(CONCAT('http://purl.uniprot.org/isoform/', ?input)),
            IRI(CONCAT('http://purl.uniprot.org/uniprot/', ?input))
        )
      ) AS ?start)
  {
    BIND(?start AS ?protein)
    ?protein a up:Protein .
  #  FILTER(sameTerm(?protein, ?start))
  }
} OFFSET 0 LIMIT 1
Fails to return results, while the equivalent where the BIND(?start AS ?protein) is commented out and the FILTER(sameTerm(?protein, ?start)) commented in does.
On http://lod.openlinksw.com/sparql/, both seem to fail the "Strict checking of void variables" check.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE proteins (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), iri VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL);
INSERT INTO proteins (id, name, iri) VALUES (1, 'Protein A', 'http://purl.uniprot.org/uniprot/P05067');
INSERT INTO proteins (id, name, iri) VALUES (2, 'Protein B', 'http://purl.uniprot.org/uniprot/P12345');
SELECT id, name, iri FROM proteins WHERE iri = CONCAT('http://purl.uniprot.org/uniprot/', 'P05067');
SELECT id, name FROM proteins WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM proteins WHERE name LIKE 'Protein%') LIMIT 1;
key_bug_features: Casting variable to IRI fails in BGP
